folio.label: 86v
folio.l: A goddis kechel , or a tryp~ of chese
Or ellis what ©ow list , we moun nat chese
A goddis halpeny , or a masse peny
Or ©eue vs of ©oure brawne , if ©e haue eny
A dagou~ of ©oure blanket , lef dame
Loo deere suster , heere wryte I ©oure name
Bacou~ or Beef , or swicú thyngö as ©e fynde
¢ A sturdy harlot , went hem ay behynde
That was here hostis man , and bar a sak
And that men ©af hem , leide it on his bak
And whan that he was , out at the dore , a noon
He planed a wey , the names euäychouö
That he byforn~ had wretyn , in his tables
He serued hem witú nyfles , and witú fables
¢ Nay there thow lixt , thow Sompnoå q¶ the frere
Pees q¶ oure host , for cristes moder deere
Telle fortú thy tale , and spare it nat at al
So thryue I q¶ this Sompnour , so I shal
So longe he went , fro hous to hous , til he
Cam to an hous , there he was wont to be
Refresshed moore , than in an hundred places
Seke lay the bonde man , whos the place is
Bedred vp on a couche , sekelowelowe seke he lay
Deus hic q¶ he , O Thomas frend good day
Seide this frere , curteisly and softe
Thomas q¶ he , god ©elde ©ow / ful ofte
Haue I vp on this benche , faren ful wele
Heere haue I eten , ful many a mery mele
And fro the benche , he drof a wey the cat
And leide a doun his potent , and his hat
And eke his skrippe , and sette hi~ softe a doune
His felawe was gon , walked in to toune
Fortú witú his knaue , in to that hostelrye
Where as he shop~ hi~ , thilke nygút to lye
O deere maister , q¶ this syke man
How han ©e faren , syn that Marche bygan
I saw ©ow nat , this fourtene nygút or more
God wot q¶ he , laboured haue I sore
And specially , for thy saluacione
Haue I seid , many a precious orisone
And for oure oæereoæere frendis , god he~ blisse
I haue to day ben , at ©oure chirche at messe
And seid a Sermoun , to my symple wytte
Nat al after the text , of holy wrytte
For it is hard to ©ow , as I suppose
And therfore wol I telle ©ow , al the glose
Glosyngö , is a ful glorious thyngö , certeyn
